DRIVE

The growth system that builds itself from context.

One Discovery assessment becomes a complete, AI-drafted growth plan — positioning, offers, referrals, credibility, and content ops — flowing across five connected tools. Nothing re-entered.

drive.brittbowman.ai
What it is

A growth operating system for independent practices.

DRIVE is five connected tools on one shared spine. A consultant onboards a practice, runs Discover, and the practice's profile and materials flow into every downstream tool — so the whole growth system drafts itself from real context, then gets refined by a human.

Discover
Assess
Profile the practice, harvest materials, score readiness.
Resolve
Align
Reconcile vocabulary into one canonical taxonomy.
Intuit
Sense
Extract buyer signals from the practice's documents.
Visualize
Build
Draft positioning, offers, referrals, and the 90-day plan.
Execute
Ship
Turn strategy into DAM-ready content operations.
What it does

One upload. Context everywhere.

The hard part of any growth engagement is re-entering the same context into every tool. DRIVE solves that with a shared spine: Discover captures the practice's profile, identity, and uploaded decks — and that context is reused everywhere downstream, drafted automatically instead of from a blank page.

Discover intake → Positioning Offer ladder Referral roster Case studies Market signals
How to use it

From assessment to activation, tool by tool.

Each tool hands its output to the next. You move left to right across the DRIVE stack, and the context you captured up front keeps compounding — nothing gets re-entered.

1

Discover — assess the practice

Profile the org, drop in their existing decks and proposals, and capture the current-state baseline. DRIVE scores readiness across the stack and recommends a track. This one assessment becomes the context for everything that follows.

Discover · current-state baseline
2

Resolve — lock the vocabulary

Before anything downstream can be trusted, the practice's language has to agree with itself. Resolve reconciles segments, personas, offers, and content types into one canonical taxonomy — the shared dictionary that Visualize and Execute both build against.

Resolve · canonical taxonomy, ready to lock
3

Intuit — read the buyer signals

Intuit reads the same uploaded transcripts, proposals, and decks and surfaces what the market is actually saying — buyer pain, objections, budget cues, competitive framing — each tagged, scored for confidence, and traced back to its source document.

Intuit · signal feed, extracted from the practice's documents
4

Visualize — the strategy drafts itself

On first open, Visualize drafts the positioning canvas and a three-tier offer ladder from the practice's own materials — real buyer language, proof points, pricing. The same materials populate the referral roster, past clients with the value delivered already filled in. The founder refines instead of starting from a blank page.

Positioning + offers
Referral roster
Visualize · both drafted from the same upload
5

Execute — ship it as content ops

The finished strategy becomes a working content plan. Execute turns every offer, persona, and channel into DAM-ready assets — each one carrying structured metadata (offer, buyer, lifecycle stage, taxonomy tags) so the practice's content operations stay governed instead of ad-hoc.

Execute · content plan with DAM-ready metadata
Who it's for

Founder-led practices where growth still runs through the founder.

✓ A strong fit

Independent advisory firms, boutique consultancies, and creative or marketing agencies — typically founder-led, referral-dependent, with positioning that lives in the founder's head and a pipeline that lurches between overbooked and anxious.

Less of a fit

Product companies without their own client engagements, or large orgs with an established growth team and a mature demand engine already in place.

DRIVE — from assessment to activation.
